Project Details

Back

Projekt

GRK 12:  Migration in Modern Europe

Subject Area Social Sciences
Term from 1995 to 2004
Project identifier Deutsche Forschungsgemeinschaft (DFG) - Project number 268931
 
  • Project Description
The subject of the research training group "Migration in Modern Europe", run by different disciplines of the social sciences, includes various aspects of migration, i.e. migration processes and migration behaviour';' migration and integration policies, forms and consequences of social inclusion or exclusion of migrants';' traditions, actual practices and rules of treatment of immigrants in different European countries. The research projects of the research training group address historical, actual and future developments and problems of migration and social integration in single European regions, member states and Europe as a whole. The research projects pay also major attention to the countries of origin of the immigrant population. The common focus of these projects are questions of ethnicity, social stratification, gender relations and cultural change. Related to this common focus the single research projects deal with problems of migration as a social process or with problems of social integration or with problems of political control and management of immigration processes and their social consequences.
DFG Programme Research Training Groups
Applicant Institution Universität Osnabrück
Participating Institution Universität Münster
Spokesperson Professor Dr. Michael Bommes (†)
Participating Researchers Professor Dr. Klaus J. Bade; Professorin Dr. Eva Breitenbach; Professor Dr. Peter Graf; Professor Dr. Siegfried Greif; Professor Dr. Utz Maas; Professor Dr. Hans-Werner Niemann; Professor Dr. Dietrich Tränhardt; Professor Dr. Albrecht Weber; Professor Dr. Hans-Joachim Wenzel
